dialogueChunks.intro: {target_npc} is back, {player_name}. And somehow, they've forgiven us.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I stood in front of the whole settlement and admitted everything: the hasty accusation based on {quest_focus}, all of it. {target_npc} just nodded and said, 'Don't let it happen again.' The trade route is secured, the {quest_item} is flowing again. {location} is stronger for having gone through this. {quest_threat} taught us a painful lesson.
dialogueChunks.outro: You showed us the truth when we didn't want to see it. I hope this is enough to show our gratitude.

playerResponses.text: The courage to admit you were wrong is its own kind of strength.
Glad {target_npc} is more forgiving than most. Take care.
Remember this next time someone brings accusations without proof.

dialogueChunks.intro: The supplies are recovered and the real culprits won't be bothering us again.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} was released and given a formal apology. {quest_stakes} is resolved: they chose to stay in {location}. The council enacted new rules: no accusations without evidence from at least two independent sources. A small change born from a lesson we won't forget. {quest_origin} will never fool us again.
dialogueChunks.outro: You kept us from making an irreversible mistake. This is yours, with the thanks of everyone who almost lost a good neighbor to a lie.

playerResponses.text: New rules are a good start. Actions will matter more than words now.
Justice done late is better than injustice done fast. Be well.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I have something to say and I don't expect it to be easy to hear.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: I was so sure {quest_focus} was real. So certain that {quest_threat} demanded action. And I was wrong about all of it. The {quest_item} we wasted could have fed families. {target_npc} came back, and the settlement is recovering, but I've had to sit with the weight of knowing how close I came to ruining an innocent life.
dialogueChunks.outro: You didn't just find the truth: you forced me to face it. Take this, and know it comes from a humbler person than the one who sent you out.

playerResponses.text: Humility earned through failure is still humility. You'll be better for it.
Next time, slow down. The truth doesn't run as fast as fear.
